data.title: Chevron to move forward with $2B expansion at Australia's Gorgon LNG
data.description:
Chevron (CVX) said Friday its Australian unit and joint venture partners reached a final investment decision to further develop the A$3 billion (~$2 billion) Gorgon Stage 3 natural gas project in Western Australia.

Chevron (CVX) said the expansion will include six wells drilled across two fields, part of a series of planned subsea tiebacks to help maintain long-term production at Gorgon, ensuring supply for the domestic Australian market and export markets.

The company plans to drill a maximum of 40 wells across seven fields with a notional field life extending to 2070, according to documents filed with Australian regulators.

Gorgon LNG has a capacity of 15.6 million tons/year of liquefied gas and 300 TJ/day of gas for the domestic Australian market; the state has a mandated 15% reservation policy for domestic use for all LNG projects.

Chevron (CVX) holds a 47% interest in the project; its partners in the venture are Exxon Mobil (XOM) and Shell (SHEL), each with a 25% stake, plus small holdings by Japan's Osaka Gas and JERA as well as U.S.-backed MidOcean.

data.title: Top SA Quant-rated communication services stocks amid NFLX-WBD blockbuster
data.description:
The media industry and communication services sector is in focus on Friday after Netflix (NFLX) announced plans to acquire Warner Bros. Discovery (WBD) for roughly $82.7B, bringing Warner Bros. studios, HBO, and HBO Max into its expanding portfolio.

Amid this blockbuster development, Seeking Alpha ranked the top 10 U.S. communication services sector stocks with at least $10B in market cap as per their Quant Ratings. 

Seeking Alpha’s Quant Ratings system grades stocks based on their relative performance on various critical quantitative measures, including valuation, growth, stock momentum, and profitability. The ratings are given on a scale from 1 to 5, with any rating above 3.5 considered bullish and any rating below 2.5 considered bearish.

Here is the list: 

No. 1: Alphabet (GOOGL), Quant Rating: 3.49, Hold. YTD Performance: +67.7%.

No. 2: Warner Bros. Discovery (WBD), Quant Rating: 3.48, Hold. YTD Performance: +132.1%.

No. 3: AT&T (T), Quant Rating: 3.42, Hold. YTD Performance: +11.5%.

No. 4: AST SpaceMobile (ASTS), Quant Rating: 3.41, Hold. YTD Performance: +244.3%.

No. 5: Verizon Communications (VZ), Quant Rating: 3.39, Hold. YTD Performance: +3.1%.

No. 6: Walt Disney Company (DIS), Quant Rating: 3.36, Hold. YTD Performance: -5.2%.

No. 7: Take-Two Interactive Software (TTWO), Quant Rating: 3.36, Hold. YTD Performance: +34.4%.

No. 8: Fox Corporation (FOXA), Quant Rating: 3.34, Hold. YTD Performance: +39.5%.

No. 9: EchoStar Corporation (SATS), Quant Rating: 3.34, Hold. YTD Performance: +225.3%. 

No. 10. Roku (ROKU), Quant Rating: 3.33, Hold. YTD Performance: +27.1%.

For more on the communication services sector, investors can also turn towards exchange traded funds for a further diversified approach to the space:

Communication Services ETFs: (XLC), (VOX), (IYZ), (RSPC), and (XTL).

data.title: DigitalBridge jumps on report Softbank in talks to acquire
data.description:
DigitalBridge (DBRG) soared 33% on a report that Softbank (OTCPK:SFTBY) is in talks to acquire the firm.

A deal could happen in the coming weeks, though deliberations are ongoing and there's no certainty talks will lead to an agreement, according to a Bloomberg report on Friday, which cited people familiar with the matter.

SoftBank and DigitalBridge declined to comment to Bloomberg.

DigitalBridge (DBRG) has a market cap of about $1.8 billion.The shares had fallen 14% this year before today.

data.title: Samsara soars as BNP Paribas suggest 'inflection' may be near
data.description:
• Samsara (IOT) shares soared more than 16% in early trading on Friday after the internet of things company reported stronger-than-expected results, leading BNP Paribas to suggest that an “inflection” point may be near.
  • “As is typically the case, management prudently kept its F4Q NNARR outlook flat y/y but assuming historical conservatism holds, it would still imply ARR growth deceleration next quarter,” BNP Paribas analyst Andre DeGasperi wrote in a note to clients. “Still, we think Samsara delivered impressive results and if trends hold, it could indicate a top-line inflection is near. As such, we remain Neutral for now until we can call this a trend, raise our near-term estimates to reflect results and maintain our $40 TP based on 12x CY26 sales.”

data.title: SentinelOne sinks following Q3 results as analysts point to soft outlook
data.description:
Cybersecurity firm SentinelOne's (S) shares had sunk 11% during Friday morning market action following its third-quarter financial results, and analysts point to a softer-than-expected outlook as the reason behind the decline.

"SentinelOne delivered its FY3Q26 results that featured beats on the top and bottom lines as the company saw strong demand for its singularity platform but will be overshadowed by its weaker outlook as management takes a prudent approach to the macro environment," said Wedbush analysts, led by Dan Ives, in a Friday investor note.

Wedbush maintained its Outperform rating on the stock but lowered its price target to $20 from $23.

"For FY26, the company expects total revenue of $1.001 billion (prior guidance of $998.0 million to $1.002 billion) vs. the Street's $1.002 billion estimate, which we see as conservative given the company's healthy pipeline and expanding customer contributions while management takes a cautious approach to the near-term macro," Ives added. "The company reduced its FY26 gross margin guidance to 78.5% (prior guide of 78.5% - 79.0%) as the compressed outlook reflects strategic investments in cloud infrastructure and capacity expansion."

Meanwhile, Morgan Stanley reiterated its Equal-weight rating and also nudged down its price target to $18 from $20.

"Our EW rating on S, despite an attractive valuation, has largely been the result of not yet seeing a positive catalyst for the name," said Morgan Stanley analysts, led by Meta Marshall, in a Friday note. "Reason being, the competitive environment in core market remains fierce, and even as the company branches out. Without a greater ability to revise estimates higher or show more profitability, the compelling valuation gets ignored."

Cybersecurity competitor CrowdStrike (CRWD) was up 2%, while Palo Alto Networks (PANW) had increased 1.8%.

data.title: Stocks in the green as NFLX-WBD acquisition raises sentiment, investors await inflation gauge
data.description:
Wall Street's major averages turned up on Friday, as traders awaited fresh inflation data in hopes of gauging the Federal Reserve's monetary policy, while news of Netflix ((NFLX)) acquiring Warner Bros. Discovery ((WBD)) dominated news.

The benchmark S&P 500 (SP500) was last +0.5%, while the Nasdaq Composite (COMP:IND) was +0.6%, and the Dow (DJI) was +0.6%. 

Streaming giant Netflix (NFLX) announced it will acquire Warner Bros. Discovery (WBD), including its film and television studios, HBO Max and HBO, for a total enterprise value of ~$82.7 billion.

“This creates basically a two-horse show. Disney (DIS), with physical assets and parks, similar to Comcast (CMCSA); and Netflix (NFLX) with tons of streaming and movie assets. Paramount (PSKY) [is] stuck now treading water. [David Ellison, CEO of Paramount Skydance] is the big loser here. Cable assets will all be shed from all. Disney needs to do the same next,” said Ross Gerber, co-founder, CEO and CIO of Gerber Kawasaki.

In economic news, core PCE inflation stalled at +0.2% MoM in September as expected. Meanwhile, December consumer sentiment was higher at 53.3 vs. 52.0 consensus and 51.0 in November, according to the initial UofM Survey.

Over in the bond market, the 10-year Treasury yield (US10Y) rose 2 basis points to 4.12%, while the 2-year yield (US2Y) rose 2 basis points to 3.55%.

data.title: Consumer sentiment improves more than expected in December
data.description:
December consumer sentiment index: 53.3 vs. 52.0 consensus and 51.0 in November, according to the initial University of Michigan Survey of Consumers data released on Friday.

Current conditions index: 50.7 vs. 51.3 consensus and 51.1 in November.

Consumer expectations index: 55.0 vs. 52.0 consensus and 51.0 prior.

Year-ahead inflation expectations: +4.1% vs. +4.5% prior.

Five-year implied inflation: +3.2% vs. +3.4% prior.

Developing... check back for updates.

data.title: Tudum! Here's what analysts are saying about Netflix's $72B deal for Warner Bros.
data.description:
Netflix (NFLX) has emerged victorious in a bidding war against Paramount Skydance (PSKY) for Warner Bros. Discovery's assets. The streaming giant on Friday made it official that it will buy the streaming and studio operations of the David Zaslav-led company in a deal with an equity value of $72B. 

WBD's board went with Netflix's bid of $27.75 per share for just the S&S unit instead of David Ellison's bid of $30 per share for the whole company. 

Seeking Alpha analyst Julia Ostian said that the deal would give the streaming giant WBD's "enormous intellectual property" but is "very risky" and "extremely expensive."  

"They’re (Netflix) taking on tens of billions of dollars in bridge loans from investment banks and paying part of the price with Netflix stock, which doesn’t surprise me, considering that their shares are quite expensive right now," Ostian said.

"After closing, the company would be heavily leveraged. Besides the money, I am really worried about the integration. Integrating two completely different cultures (and generations of doing business) is incredibly difficult, and Netflix is taking a huge risk of alienating subscribers or losing its identity in the process. I personally wouldn’t want to hold the stock while all of this plays out," Ostian added.

Seeking Alpha analyst YR Research said Netflix's $72B deal is "a bold step towards a much bigger future than it ever could have achieved without Warner Bros but is risking near-term regulatory and talent scrutiny."

"If it succeeds, I expect Netflix's future as a $1 trillion company is all but certain," YR Research said.

"My first reaction is, watch out, Netflix," said François Godard, media and telecoms analyst at Enders Analysis. "The merger of Warner Bros. and Discovery destroyed value, and the risk is that this new deal will also produce a result smaller than the addition of the parts. HBO, the most brilliant TV creative house, survived Zaslav, but will it die under Netflix?"

On Thursday, Wedbush said significant concerns remain within the industry and among government officials about the NFLX + WBD combination.

"Should the exclusive negotiations with Netflix lead to a proposed sale, the regulatory processwould be lengthy and difficult and may ultimately block the sale without clear assurances from the studio side," Wedbush said.

Prior to the deal announcement, Seeking Alpha analyst Daniel Jones said if there is any time to make a big acquisition like this, it's now, due to the business-friendly views of the current political leadership in the U.S., but he would rather stay away from buying WBD.

Analysts at Deutsche Bank spoke to 25-year DOJ veteran Lawrence Frankel before the deal was announced, who said that regardless of who the buyer is, be it Netflix, Paramount, or Comcast (CMCSA), the deal will eventually clear, even if the DOJ were to sue to block a proposed combination.

data.title: Hassett sees 25-bp rate cut, backs Bessent's proposal on Fed governance
data.description:
Kevin Hassett, the White House's National Economic Council chief, on Friday urged the Federal Reserve to lower interest rates and predicted a quarter-point reduction at next week's policy meeting.

Speaking in an interview with Fox Business, Hassett said, “I think we should, and I think that we are likely to" cut rates. Fed governors and regional chiefs "now seem much more like they’re leaning in the direction of a rate cut,” he added.

Fed funds futures traders also largely expect a 25-basis-point rate cut at the Dec. 9-10 meeting, according to the CME FedWatch tool, aligning with the view of Hassett, who is the top candidate to success Jerome Powell next year as Fed chair.

On Friday, Hassett said he supported Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent's call to change how regional Federal Reserve presidents are selected. Bessent, amid his broader effort to reshape Fed leadership, said he will "start advocating going forward, not retroactively, that regional Fed presidents must have lived in their district for at least three years," a proposal that may require approval from Congress.

Hassett said: “The unfortunate thing about the current design of the Federal Reserve is that the only folks who always get a vote on interest rates are the people who live in Washington and the people who live in New York." He added that he and Bessent had talked about changing that. 

“I think it wouldn’t require anybody to go in and fire anybody who’s there now,” NEC head said in a separate Fox Business interview.

Dear readers: We recognize that politics often intersects with the financial news of the day, so we invite you to click here to join the separate political discussion.

data.title: SK Hynix chief says AI not in bubble, but stocks might face correction: report
data.description:
Chey Tae-won, chair of SK Group, the conglomerate that owns the prolific memory maker SK Hynix (OTCPK:HXSC.F), said during a forum in Seoul that the artificial intelligence industry is not in a bubble, according to Reuters.

However, investor exuberance has led to sky-high valuations, which might face some corrections.

"But when you look at the stock markets, they rose too fast and too much, and I think it is natural that there could be some period of corrections," he said, according to the report.

SK Hynix provides memory chips to Nvidia (NVDA), the Magnificent Seven member that has benefited greatly from the rise of AI due to its powerful graphics processing units. In October, the South Korea-based company reported record-high revenue and has already sold out its memory chip production for the year ahead due to the rise of AI.

Multiple memory companies have experienced especially strong gains during 2025. Micron Technology (MU) shares have surged 175% year to date, while Seagate Technology (STX) has rocketed more than 200%. 

Broadcom (AVGO) has increased nearly 70% year to date, and Advanced Micro Devices (AMD) shares have climbed 81%. Nvidia shares have advanced 36%. Intel (INTC) shares have more than doubled in 2025.

Some software companies have also enjoyed the AI wave. Palantir (PLTR) has shot up 135%, while AppLovin (APP) has risen 116%.
